title: Parole rate is up in Mass., including for people with life sentences
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts parole rates increased 3.5% in 2024, influenced by mandatory release and medical releases.
Life sentence parole rates rose from 57% in 2023 to 72% in 2024, partly due to the Mattis decision.
In 2024, 1,280 people were on parole with 400 serving life sentences, according to Parole Board data.

title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo of Rosalie Miller, a 1997 murder victim, is released to seek new witnesses in the cold case.
Miller was last seen on December 8, 1996, in Manchester, N.H. Her body was found in Auburn in January 1997.
Authorities request information from anyone who had contact with Miller or saw her near Londonderry Turnpike in December 1996.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, is charged with murder for allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
After the shooting, Sousa allegedly caused a car crash in Swansea, Mass., killing a man and a woman in a Subaru Ascent.
Sousa's Infiniti, containing a pistol and ammunition, crashed on Route 136; he's hospitalized and expected to survive.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are considering trading for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, a 26-year-old top-line player.
Acquiring Thomas would require Bruins to part with key assets, including draft picks and prospects like Dean Letourneau.
St. Louis Blues hold leverage in Thomas trade talks, and may wait for a favorable offer before making any decisions.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Maine state approved a $3,000 settlement without guilt admission for a New York couple accused of killing trees.
Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett allegedly poisoned trees in Rockport, Maine, to improve their waterfront view.
Despite the settlement, Antonson and Hackett neither apologized nor admitted to poisoning their neighbor's trees.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: KC Concepcion and Omar Cooper stand out as wide receiver prospects for Patriots in 2026 NFL Draft.
Romello Height and Dani Dennis-Sutton are potential Day 2 edge rusher picks for Patriots.
Max Iheanachor could be a viable first-round offensive tackle pick for Patriots.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots unlikely to trade major draft capital for star players, not viewing themselves as 'one player away'.
Team linked to trade interests in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby, but prices are high.
Patriots prioritize drafting and developing over making major trades; hold 11 picks in 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power received state approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage system at a former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The facility will house 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures, covering 16.5 acres, and include two substations and transmission lines.
The project is part of the 'Docklands' development, featuring manufacturing, lab, office, retail, and residential spaces in its second phase.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted the Feb. 28 'SNL' with Mumford & Sons as the musical guest.
Storrie appeared with 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams, both featured in a skating-themed sketch.
US Olympic hockey players, including Megan Keller and Hilary Knight, joined Storrie during the monologue.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 1-0 to New York Red Bulls with a goal from 17-year-old Julian Hall.
Center-backs Brayan Ceballos and Mamadou Fofana returned to strengthen Revolution's defense against Red Bulls' possession-heavy play.
Revolution struggled with Red Bulls' pressing, failing to capitalize on rare second-half opportunities, including a chance by Jackson Yueill.
